summary_en: Ordinary dividend raised 106→118 yen/share (+12 yen). Special dividend 15 yen. Full-year total 220 yen/share (prior forecast 208 yen). Interim dividend 87 yen already paid. Payout ratio (ordinary only) 40%+ per 3-year mid-term plan target.
title_en: Notice of Dividend Forecast Revision (Dividend Increase) for Fiscal Year Ending March 2026
translation_en_brief:
Dainichiseika Color & Chemicals announced on 15 May 2026 a revision to its FY3/26 (year ending 31 March 2026) dividend forecast. The ordinary dividend for the period-end payment has been increased from the prior forecast of 106 yen/share to 118 yen/share, a rise of 12 yen. The special dividend remains at 15 yen/share. Combined with the interim dividend of 87 yen/share (72 yen ordinary + 15 yen special) already paid, the full-year dividend totals 220 yen/share (190 yen ordinary + 30 yen special), compared to the prior forecast of 208 yen/share.

The revision reflects the company's 3-year mid-term plan ("明日への変革 2027") which targets a minimum ordinary payout ratio of 40% (excluding special dividends) and a 3-year average total payout ratio of 50% or more. The company also committed to an annual special dividend of 30 yen/share across four fiscal years (FY3/25–FY3/28, totaling approximately 2 billion yen) funded by gains from the August 2024 sale of its former Kawaguchi manufacturing facility.

The revision is subject to shareholder approval at the 123rd Ordinary General Meeting scheduled for 26 June 2026.
